mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and signals easier financial conditions, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the cut is part of an easing cycle.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in most periods when FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, or if the sign reverses during easing cycles in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in CPIAUCSL above recent trend forces rate-hike expectations upward, compresses equity valuations, and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation trend rises above its 6-month average by more than 0.4 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-threshold episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal has negative predictive power after controlling for the prior 20-day SPX move.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE with falling PAYEMS indicates a labor-market deterioration that foreshadows weaker household income and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while PAYEMS declines over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint unemployment/payroll deterioration signal fails to produce negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if either proxy alone explains the same effect with no incremental value from the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS reflects stronger consumer spending and revenue growth, which supports earnings expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if rising retail sales do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns in out-of-sample tests, or if the sign becomes neutral or negative for consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rebound in INDPRO signals improving industrial output and cyclical activity, which lifts earnings-sensitive sectors and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for two consecutive monthly releases after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if industrial-production rebounds are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns in a majority of triggered cases, or if the effect disappears once prior market trend is included.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rises together with RSAFS, the economy is signaling synchronized housing and consumption momentum, which supports broad risk assets and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined housing-sales signal does not outperform each proxy alone, or if positive 20-day SPX returns do not concentrate in periods where both series are above their recent trends.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 3.6% with payroll growth still positive indicates a tight but not yet collapsing labor market, which supports earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE stays below 3.6% while PAYEMS remains above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the low-unemployment/high-payroll regime fails to generate positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect vanishes once monthly market volatility is elevated.

mechanism_narrative: A stable or falling FEDFUNDS rate after an inflation peak keeps real-rate pressure from rising further, supporting valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation has stopped accelerating and FEDFUNDS is flat or down versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if post-inflation-peak periods with stable or lower FEDFUNDS do not show positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal becomes negative when inflation is decelerating.
